title: Production possibility curves
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS — PPC diagrams, opportunity cost, efficiency, and economic growth with GeoGebra production possibility frontier.
learningObjectives: Draw and interpret production possibility curves.
Explain opportunity cost using PPC slope.
Show economic growth and specialisation on PPC diagrams.

simpleExplanation.title: Choosing what to produce
simpleExplanation.summary: A PPC shows maximum combinations of two goods with fixed resources. The slope is the opportunity cost of one good in terms of the other.
steps: Points on the curve — productive efficiency (full employment of resources).
Inside the curve — unemployed resources or inefficiency.
Outward shift — economic growth (more resources or better technology).

sections.content: The **PPC** is the standard 9708 diagram for **scarcity**, **choice**, and **opportunity cost** (links to 1.1). Examiners expect **bowed-out** curves when OC increases.

sections.question: A country moves from a point inside the PPC to a point on the PPC. What has changed?
sections.solution:
**Productive efficiency improved** — previously unemployed resources are now used.

Output of at least one good increases without reducing the other (or both increase).

sections.content: When OC rises along a bowed PPC, explain **specialisation** — resources are not equally suited to producing both goods.
